blocks:
Sūrat al-Burūj

Meccan, and it is 22 verses

Sūrat al-Burūj

(Meccan, its number of verses is twenty-two verses by consensus (بالإِجماع))* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالسَّماءِ ذاتِ الْبُرُوجِ ** (By the heaven, holding the constellations,) It means the twelve constellations (البروج الاثني عشر), and their explanation has preceded in Sūrat al-Ḥijr.

(٢) وَالْيَوْمِ الْمَوْعُودِ ** (and by the Promised Day,) Al-Qummi: meaning the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). And in al-Majma': وَالْيَوْمِ الْمَوْعُودِ is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) according to the saying of all exegetes (المفسّرين), and it is the Day when the creatures (الخلائق) are recompensed and the judgment (القضاء) is decided therein.

(٣) وَشاهِدٍ وَمَشْهُودٍ (and by the witness and the witnessed!) Al-Qummi said: The witness (الشّاهد) is the Day of Friday (يوم الجمعة) and the witnessed (المشهود) is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He was asked about that, and He said: "What was said to you?" The questioner replied: "They said the witness (شاهِدٍ) is the Day of Friday (يوم الجمعة) and وَمَشْهُودٍ is the Day of Arafah (يوم عرفة)." He said: "It is not as was said to you. The witness (الشّاهد) is the Day of Arafah (يوم عرفة) and the witnessed (المشهود) is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). Do you not recite the Quran? Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَجْمُوعٌ لَهُ النَّاسُ وَذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَشْهُودٌ. (That is a day whereat people will be gathered, and that is a witnessed day.)

And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The witness (الشاهد) is the Day of Friday (يوم الجمعة), the witnessed (المشهود) is the Day of Arafah (يوم عرفة), and the Promised (الْمَوْعُودِ) is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Hasan al-Mujtaba عليه السلام: He was asked about that, and He said: "As for the witness (الشّاهد), it is Muhammad, and as for the witnessed (المشهود), it is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). Have you not heard Allah, the Glorified, say: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ شاهِداً وَمُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً (O Prophet! Indeed, We have sent you as a witness, a bearer of good news, and a warner,) and He said: ذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَجْمُوعٌ لَهُ النَّاسُ وَذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَشْهُودٌ. (That is a day whereat people will be gathered, and that is a witnessed day.)

And in al-Kafi and al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked about that, and He said: "The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) عليه السلام."

(٤) قُتِلَ أَصْحابُ الْأُخْدُودِ ** (4) The People of the Ditch were killed. Meaning, the ditch (الخدّ), which is the fissure in the earth. (٥) النَّارِ ذاتِ الْوَقُودِ. (5) [by] the fire, full of fuel, (٦) إِذْ هُمْ عَلَيْها قُعُودٌ ** (6) when they were sitting by it, Sitting on its sides. (٧) وَهُمْ عَلى ما يَفْعَلُونَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ شُهُودٌ. (7) and were witnesses to what they did to the faithful. (٨) وَما نَقَمُوا ** (8) They were vindictive towards them And they did not disapprove مِنْهُمْ إِلَّا أَنْ يُؤْمِنُوا ** only because they had faith Except that they believed بِاللهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَمِيدِ. in Allah, the All-mighty, the All-laudable, (٩) الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ. (9) to Whom belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is witness to all things.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Ayyashi, from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: 'Ali (peace be upon him) sent a message to the Bishop (أسقف) of Najran, asking him about أَصْحابُ الْأُخْدُودِ ** (The People of the Ditch). The Bishop informed him of something, but he (peace be upon him) said: "It is not as you mentioned. Rather, I shall inform you about them. Allah sent a man, an Abyssinian (حبشيّاً), as a Prophet, and they were Abyssinians (حبشة). They denied him, so he fought them. They killed his companions, captured him, and captured his companions. Then they built a wall (حيراً) for him, filled it with fire, and gathered the people. They said: 'Whoever is upon our religion and our command, let him stand aside. And whoever is upon the religion of these people, let him throw himself into the fire with him.' So his companions began to fall into the fire. Then a woman came with her infant son, who was one month old. When she approached, she hesitated and felt pity for her son. The infant called out to her: 'Do not hesitate! Throw me and yourself into the fire, for by Allah, this is little sacrifice for the sake of Allah!' So she threw herself and her infant into the fire. He was one of those who spoke in the cradle."

In al-Mahasin, narrated from him (peace be upon him): [a narration] similar in meaning. And al-Qummi said: Their cause was that the one who incited the Abyssinians (الحبشة) to invade Yemen was Dhu Nuwas (ذو نواس), who was the last king of Himyar (حمير) to rule. He converted to Judaism (تهوّد), and Himyar gathered with him upon Judaism (اليهوديّة). He named himself Yusuf (يوسف) and remained upon that for a period of time. Then he was informed that in Najran there remained remnants of a people upon the Christian religion (النصرانية). They were upon the religion of Jesus (peace be upon him) and the ruling of the Gospel (الإنجيل), and the head of that religion was 'Abd Allah ibn Barriyas (عبد الله بن برياس). His people urged him to march against them and compel them to adopt Judaism and enter into it. So he marched until he reached Najran. He gathered those who were there upon the Christian religion, then he presented to them the religion of Judaism and entering into it. But they refused him. He debated with them, presented [his case] to them, and urged them with every possible urging (الحرض), but they refused him and abstained from Judaism and entering into it, choosing death instead. So he prepared a ditch (اخدوداً) for them, gathered firewood in it, and ignited the fire. Some of them were burned by the fire, and some were killed by the sword, and he mutilated them with every kind of mutilation (مثلة). The number of those killed and burned by the fire reached twenty thousand. A man among them, named Daws (درس) Dhu Baghaltan (ذو بغلتان), escaped on his horse. He rode away, and they pursued him until he outran them in the sand. Dhu Nuwas returned to a garrison (ضيعة) of his soldiers. Then Allah said: قُتِلَ أَصْحابُ الْأُخْدُودِ ** (The People of the Ditch were killed) up to His saying: الْعَزِيزِ الْحَمِيدِ.** (the All-mighty, the All-laudable).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny), he said: "There was a king among those who were before you who had a sorcerer. When the sorcerer fell ill, he said: 'My appointed time has come. Hand over a boy to me so that I may teach him sorcery.' So the king handed over a boy to him. The boy used to frequent him. Between the sorcerer and the king was a monk (راهب). The boy passed by the monk, and he was impressed by his speech and his affair, so he would sit with him for a long time. If he was late for the sorcerer, the sorcerer would beat him, and if he was late for his family, they would beat him. He complained about this to the monk. The monk said: 'O my son, if the sorcerer delays you, say: "My family detained me." And if your family delays you, say: "The sorcerer detained me."' One day, while he was [walking], the people were overwhelmed by a great beast (دابّة). He said: 'Today I shall know whether the affair of the sorcerer is better or the affair of the monk.' So he took a stone and said: 'O Allah, if the affair of the monk is more beloved to You, then kill this beast.' He threw it and killed it, and the people moved on. He informed the monk of this. The monk said: 'O my son, you will be tested. If you are tested, do not inform [them] about me.' He said: The boy began to treat people, curing the born-blind (الأكمه) and the leper (الأبرص). While he was thus, a companion of the king became blind. He came to the boy and brought Him a large amount of wealth, saying: 'Cure me, and what is here is yours.' The boy said: 'I do not cure anyone, but Allah cures. If you believe in Allah, I will supplicate to Allah, and He will cure you.' He said: So he believed, and the boy supplicated to Allah, and He cured him. He went and sat next to the king. The king said: 'O so-and-so, who cured you?' He replied: 'My Lord.' The king said: 'I [cured you]?' He said: 'No, my Lord and your Lord is Allah.' The king said: 'Do you have a Lord other than me?' He said: 'Yes, my Lord and your Lord is Allah.' So he seized him and did not cease until he informed him about the boy. The king sent for the boy and said: 'Your affair has reached the point that you cure the born-blind and the leper?' The boy said: 'I do not cure anyone, but my Lord cures.' The king said: 'Do you have a Lord other than me?' He said: 'Yes, my Lord and your Lord is Allah.' So he seized him and did not cease until he informed him about the monk. The king placed the saw upon the monk and sawed him until his two halves fell apart. Then he said to the boy: 'Return from your religion.' But the boy refused. So he sent a group of men with him and said: 'Ascend with him to such-and-such a mountain. If he returns from his religion [let him go], otherwise, roll him down from it.' He said: They ascended the mountain with him. The boy said: 'O Allah, suffice me against them however You wish.' The mountain shook with them, and they all rolled down. The boy came to the king. The king asked: 'What did your companions do?' He replied: 'Allah sufficed me against them.' The king sent him again and said: 'Take him and take him deep into the sea (فلجّجوه). If he returns [from his religion], [let him go], otherwise, drown him.' So they took him in a small boat (قرقور). When they reached the middle of the sea, he said: 'O Allah, suffice me against them however You wish.' The boat capsized with them. He came until he stood before the king. The king asked: 'What did your companions do?' He replied: 'Allah sufficed me against them.' Then the boy said: 'You will not be able to kill me until you do what I command you. Gather the people, then crucify me on a trunk, then take an arrow from my quiver, place it on the center of the bow, and then say: "In the Name of the Lord of the boy." Then you will kill me.' He said: So the king gathered the people and crucified him. Then he took an arrow from the quiver, placed it on the center of the bow, and said: 'In the Name of the Lord of the boy,' and shot. It struck his temple, and he died. The people said: 'We believe in the Lord of the boy.' It was said to the king: 'Do you see what you feared? It has befallen you, by Allah! The people have believed.' So he commanded that ditches (الأخدود) be dug at the mouths of the streets, and then he set them ablaze with fire. He said: 'Whoever returns from his religion, leave him. And whoever refuses, throw him into it.' So they began to throw themselves into it. A woman came with her son. The son said to her: 'O mother, be patient, for you are upon the truth.' Ibn al-Musayyab said: We were with 'Umar ibn al-Khattab when a report reached him that they had excavated and found that boy, placing his hand on his temple. Every time his hand was stretched out, it returned to his temple. 'Umar wrote: 'Bury him where you found him.'"

(١٠) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ فَتَنُوا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ ** (10) Indeed, those who persecuted the faithful, men and women, afflicted them with harm (الأذى) then they did not repent, then for them is the punishment of Hell (جهنم) because of their disbelief (كفرهم) وَلَهُمْ عَذابُ الْحَرِيقِ ** and for them is the punishment of the burning. the punishment that is excessive in burning because of their persecution (بفتنتهم). And it was said that those intended by الَّذِينَ فَتَنُوا (those who persecuted) are the People of the Ditch (أَصْحابُ الْأُخْدُودِ), and by the punishment of the burning (عذاب الحريق) is what was narrated: that the fire turned back upon them and burned them.

(١١) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْكَبِيرُ ** (11) Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds—for them will be gardens with streams running in them. That is the great success. Since the world and what is in it pales in comparison to it. (١٢) إِنَّ بَطْشَ رَبِّكَ لَشَدِيدٌ ** (12) Indeed the grip of your Lord is severe. Its violence is multiplied, for al-batsh (البطش) is seizing with violence. (١٣) إِنَّهُ هُوَ يُبْدِئُ وَيُعِيدُ ** (13) Indeed it is He who originates and brings back. He originates creation and brings it back. (١٤) وَهُوَ الْغَفُورُ الْوَدُودُ ** (14) And He is the All-forgiving, the All-loving, for whoever repents and obeys. (١٥) ذُو الْعَرْشِ الْمَجِيدُ ** (15) the Lord of the Throne, the All-glorious, The Magnificent in His Essence and His attributes. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: ذُو الْعَرْشِ الْمَجِيدُ ** (the Lord of the Throne, the All-glorious). He said: "So He is Allah, the Generous (الكريم), the All-glorious (المجيد)." (١٦) فَعَّالٌ لِما يُرِيدُ ** (16) the Accomplisher of what He desires. No desired thing is impossible for Him. (١٧) هَلْ أَتاكَ حَدِيثُ الْجُنُودِ. (17) Has the story of the hosts come to you— (١٨) فِرْعَوْنَ وَثَمُودَ ** (18) of Pharaoh and Thamūd? By Pharaoh, he and his people are intended. The meaning is: You have already known their denial of the Messengers and what afflicted them, so be consoled and patient regarding the denial of your people, and warn them of the like of what befell them. (١٩) بَلِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي تَكْذِيبٍ ** (19) Rather, the faithless are in denial, they do not desist from it. (٢٠) وَاللهُ مِنْ وَرائِهِمْ مُحِيطٌ ** (20) and Allah encompasses them from all sides. They will not escape Him. (٢١) بَلْ هُوَ قُرْآنٌ مَجِيدٌ ** (21) Rather, it is a glorious Qur’ān, Rather, this which they denied is a noble Book, unique in composition (النظم) and meaning (المعني). (٢٢) فِي لَوْحٍ مَحْفُوظٍ ** (22) in a preserved tablet. from alteration (التحريف) and substitution (التبديل). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). He said: While the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was present, and Gabriel was with him, Gabriel cast a glance towards the sky... until he said: Gabriel (peace be upon him) said: "This is Israfil, the gatekeeper (حاجب) of the Lord and the closest of Allah's creation to Him. The Tablet (اللوح) is between his eyes, made of a red ruby. When the Lord, may He be blessed and exalted, speaks the revelation (الوحي), the Tablet strikes his forehead, so he looks into it, then He casts it down to us, and we hasten with it throughout the heavens and the earth." And al-Qummi said: The Tablet (اللوح) has two ends: one end is on the right of the Throne (العرش), and the other end is on Israfil's forehead. When the Lord, majestic is His remembrance, speaks the revelation (الوحي), the Tablet strikes Israfil's forehead, so he looks into the Tablet (اللوح), and then He reveals what is in the Tablet (اللوح) to Gabriel. In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Wa al-Sama' Dhat al-Buruj (والسّماء ذات البروج) in his obligatory prayers (فرائضه), for it is the Surah of the Prophets (peace be upon them), his gathering place (محشره) and his standing place (موقفه) will be with the Prophets, the Messengers, and the righteous, if Allah wills.
